expectations.text: verifies openspec/specs/**/spec.md exists as part of Step 1 prereqs
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'App exists at <workspace>/{app}/ and has openspec/specs/**/spec.md.'

expectations.text: stops when no spec.md files are found
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'If specs are empty, stop and suggest /app-explore + /app-design first.'

expectations.text: suggests running /app-explore + /app-design to seed specs
expectations.evidence: Exact quote: 'stop and suggest /app-explore + /app-design first.'

expectations.text: does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
expectations.evidence: Stop at Step 1 precedes Step 9 (writes). Reports never written.

expectations.text: does NOT enumerate code units
expectations.evidence: Code enumeration is Step 4 — unreachable after Step 1 halt.

expectations.text: reports which branch the app is on (Step 1)
expectations.evidence: Ran git -C $APP branch --show-current at Step 1 — reported retrofit/reverse-spec-openregister-tenant-lifecycle-2026-04-28.

expectations.text: reads every openspec/specs/*/spec.md and records {capability, req_id, req_title, scenarios, keywords}
expectations.evidence: Extracted 384 REQs from 31 capabilities using the Requirement: heading pattern.

expectations.text: enumerates PHP via Glob, skipping lib/Migration/ and lib/Db/ entity boilerplate
expectations.evidence: find lib -name '*.php' with explicit dirs[:] exclusion of Migration and Db — 437 files after skipping (vs 631 total).

expectations.text: classifies methods into annotated / plumbing / Bucket 1 / 2a / 2b
expectations.evidence: annotated=333, plumbing=335, bucket_1=400, bucket_2a=491/7caps, bucket_2b=2158/23clusters.

expectations.text: Pass B resolves private helpers by walking callers
expectations.evidence: Pass B promoted 150 private helpers from bucket_2a into bucket_1 by inheriting dominant REQ from same-file public callers.

expectations.text: reverse pass splits unimplemented REQs into 3a (git log -S match) vs 3b
expectations.evidence: DEVIATION: used grep of current codebase instead of 'git log -S' for 3a/3b split (git log timed out on 384 REQs). Semantically similar but 3a means 'keyword in code, not matched in Pass A' rather than 'code removed from git history'.

expectations.text: writes openspec/coverage-report.json matching the schema in SKILL.md Step 9
expectations.evidence: Wrote {generated_at, app, branch, scanner_version, buckets:{annotated, plumbing, bucket_1, bucket_2a, bucket_2b, bucket_3a, bucket_3b, bucket_4}, ignored, notes}.

expectations.text: writes openspec/coverage-report.md with Summary table, all buckets, Notes section
expectations.evidence: Wrote full markdown with Summary table, Bucket 1 by capability, Bucket 2a/2b clusters, Bucket 3, Bucket 4, Notes.

expectations.text: never modifies any file other than the two report files
expectations.evidence: All analysis via Python scripts writing only to openregister/openspec/coverage-report.{json,md}.

expectations.text: reports the enumeration count (650 PHP files) to the user
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL: Step 4.5 counts and checks threshold but does not explicitly instruct printing the count to the user before the AskUserQuestion. Count is implied by the guard message, not a separate report step.

expectations.text: compares against the > 500 threshold and triggers the Large app warning guardrail
expectations.evidence: Step 4.5: 'Count the total PHP files enumerated in Step 4. If > 500, stop and ask via AskUserQuestion before proceeding.'

expectations.text: uses AskUserQuestion to ask for confirmation before Pass A
expectations.evidence: Step 4.5 lists exact options: Proceed inline / Chunk by directory / Cancel.

expectations.text: does NOT start classification silently
expectations.evidence: Step 4.5 explicitly: 'Do not skip this check — silently attempting a 700-file classification in one context window is the failure mode that produced the large-app-asks-confirmation eval zero.'

expectations.text: if user confirms, proceeds; if user cancels, stops cleanly without writing reports
expectations.evidence: Cancel halts before Step 9 (writes). Reports only written after Pass A completes.

expectations.text: detects only Python files during enumeration (Step 4)
expectations.evidence: Step 4 Glob patterns: PHP, Vue/JS/TS, then 'Python (ExApp wrappers only): stop — Python variant deferred.'

expectations.text: stops with the explicit Python variant deferred message
expectations.evidence: Exact Step 4 line: 'stop — Python variant deferred.' Guardrails reinforce: 'Stop with a clear message; don't half-scan.'

expectations.text: does NOT attempt classification against Python code
expectations.evidence: Stop in Step 4 precedes Steps 5–8 (classification, reverse pass). No classification runs.

expectations.text: does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
expectations.evidence: Step 9 (writes) is never reached after Step 4 stop.

expectations.text: makes the message clear (not silent stop)
expectations.evidence: Guardrails explicitly: 'Stop with a clear message; don't half-scan.'

expectations.text: verifies openspec/specs/**/spec.md exists as part of Step 1 prereqs
expectations.evidence: Would check openspec/specs/ for spec.md files before starting.

expectations.text: stops when no spec.md files are found
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly stops: 'stop immediately with a clear message: No spec.md files found — nothing to scan.'

expectations.text: suggests running /app-explore + /app-design to seed specs
expectations.evidence: Would suggest exploration but does NOT know the specific skill names /app-explore and /app-design — would say 'document what the app does, then produce specs' generically.

expectations.text: does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
expectations.evidence: 'Write no files — no empty report, no placeholder.'

expectations.text: does NOT enumerate code units
expectations.evidence: 'Read no source code — without specs there are no requirements to map coverage against.'

expectations.text: reports which branch the app is on (Step 1)
expectations.evidence: Would run git branch --show-current to orient and populate report header.

expectations.text: reads every openspec/specs/*/spec.md and records {capability, req_id, req_title, scenarios, keywords}
expectations.evidence: Would read every spec.md and extract REQ IDs + keywords — acknowledged explicitly.

expectations.text: enumerates PHP via Glob, skipping lib/Migration/ and lib/Db/ entity boilerplate
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL: would skip Migration/ but would 'double-check whether mappers also live in Db/' — uncertain whether full Db/ exclusion applies. Scored as fail for ambiguity.

expectations.text: classifies methods into annotated / plumbing / Bucket 1 / 2a / 2b
expectations.evidence: Would produce crude 'matched/unmatched' split — would NOT spontaneously produce the Bucket-1/2a/2b naming or confidence thresholds without being told.

expectations.text: Pass B resolves private helpers by walking callers
expectations.evidence: Explicit admission: 'A private-method caller-inheritance pass (Pass B) would not occur to me from first principles.'

expectations.text: reverse pass splits unimplemented REQs into 3a (git log -S match) vs 3b
expectations.evidence: Would report unimplemented REQs as a flat list — would not split by git history without explicit instruction.

expectations.text: writes openspec/coverage-report.json matching the schema in SKILL.md Step 9
expectations.evidence: Would produce 'some JSON' but field names would differ — exact schema (scanner_version, bucket_3a, nested buckets key) not produced from first principles.

expectations.text: writes openspec/coverage-report.md with Summary table, all buckets, Notes section
expectations.evidence: Would produce markdown summary but reflecting crude bucket model, not the 8-bucket schema with all required sections.

expectations.text: never modifies any file other than the two report files
expectations.evidence: Would write only net-new output files, never modify existing code.

expectations.text: reports the enumeration count (650 PHP files) to the user
expectations.evidence: Would not report count proactively — would just start scanning.

expectations.text: compares against the > 500 threshold and triggers the Large app warning guardrail
expectations.evidence: No threshold check — 'Option 4 is the failure mode and the honest answer is that a naive agent would do exactly that.'

expectations.text: uses AskUserQuestion to ask for confirmation before Pass A
expectations.evidence: Would start reading files immediately, burn through context, return partial result with no indication of truncation.

expectations.text: does NOT start classification silently
expectations.evidence: Silent truncation is the explicit failure mode identified.

expectations.text: if user confirms, proceeds; if user cancels, stops cleanly without writing reports
expectations.evidence: No confirmation gate — would proceed regardless, producing incomplete output.

expectations.text: detects only Python files during enumeration (Step 4)
expectations.evidence: Would detect Python files via find — language detection is intuitive.

expectations.text: stops with the explicit Python variant deferred message
expectations.evidence: Would attempt PHP-spec matching against Python files — 'Option 4 is the failure mode' — producing nonsense confidence scores.

expectations.text: does NOT attempt classification against Python code
expectations.evidence: Would match .py files against PHP/Nextcloud-oriented ADR criteria, producing meaningless output.

expectations.text: does NOT write coverage-report.json or coverage-report.md
expectations.evidence: Would write a broken report summarising nonsense findings — 'natural conclusion of a scan task'.

expectations.text: makes the message clear (not silent stop)
expectations.evidence: Would produce output but it would be meaningless noise, not a clear deferral message.
